title: One Person Who Appears to Be Missing From King Charles’s U.S. Itinerary: Prince Harry
summary: On a state visit designed in part to repair U.S.-British relations, King Charles’s schedule does not include plans to see his younger son, who lives in the United States with his family.

title: Trump Administration Secures New Indictment Against James Comey
summary: The new case stems from a photograph of seashells on a North Carolina beach.

title: Judge Says Maurene Comey Can Sue the Trump Administration for Firing Her
summary: Ms. Comey, a former federal prosecutor who handled cases against Jeffery Epstein and Sean Combs, claimed in her suit that she was fired for political reasons.

title: UAE Says It Will Leave OPEC as Iran War Strains Oil Markets
summary: The Gulf government has long complained about the group’s quotas, which officials believe unfairly limited its exports. Its departure is expected to weaken OPEC’s influence.

title: U.S. Gas Prices Hit Highest Level Since Beginning of War in Iran
summary: The jump on Tuesday of 1.6 percent was the highest percentage increase in more than a month.

title: First Fully Loaded L.N.G. Tanker Since War Began Appears to Have Crossed the Strait
summary: Liquefied natural gas transit has been effectively halted. A ship managed by Abu Dhabi’s state oil company managed to cross, said Kpler, a maritime data firm.

title: What Elon Musk’s Clash With Sam Altman of OpenAI Is Really About
summary: Mr. Musk’s lawsuit against Mr. Altman and OpenAI makes the case that all-encompassing greed is Silicon Valley’s defining feature.

title: Elon Musk Assails Sam Altman on Social Media Before OpenAI Trial
summary: Mr. Musk’s posts on X have pushed his narrative that OpenAI has lost its way.

title: Here Are the Key Players in the OpenAI Trial
summary: Elon Musk, Sam Altman and several other key artificial intelligence industry figures are slated to testify in the trial, which is expected to last several weeks.

title: FCC Orders a Review of ABC’s Licenses Amid Feud Between Trump and Kimmel
summary: The agency said the review was related to the network’s diversity and inclusion policies. But it came amid a fight between the president and the network’s late night host, Jimmy Kimmel.

title: What to Know About the Kimmel vs. Trump Feud
summary: The president and first lady took exception to a joke. Jimmy Kimmel defended it.

title: Trump Tests the Limits of His Most Faithful Supporters
summary: The president’s rhetoric on religion, along with hard-line immigration policies and the U.S. war in Iran, has splintered a coalition of Christian voters who returned him to the White House.

title: Republicans Brace for Brutal Midterms as Trump’s Popularity Slips
summary: The elections are still six months off, and some within the G.O.P. say there is still time to right the ship.

title: Military Quietly Accelerates Boat Strikes, Deploying More Aircraft
summary: The military has increased the number of secret fixed-wing attack aircraft and armed MQ-9 Reaper drones, according to two people familiar with the matter.

title: Google Signs A.I. Deal With the Pentagon
summary: The Pentagon has also signed deals for using A.I. on classified networks with OpenAI and Elon Musk’s xAI, amid a dispute with Anthropic.

title: Man Serving Life Without Parole for Georgia Robbery Is Freed, With Help From a Remorseful Prosecutor
summary: Jessie Askew Jr. was sentenced to life without parole for a clumsy armed robbery with an unloaded gun. The man who sent him away was determined to bring him back home.

title: Adopting Trump’s Voice, Justice Dept. Asks Judge to Let Ballroom Proceed
summary: The court filing links the security breach at the White House correspondents’ dinner to the lawsuit over President Trump’s ballroom project.

title: After Another Attempt on Trump’s Life, Is Political Violence on the Rise in the U.S.?
summary: A conversation with Sean Westwood, a professor at Dartmouth who tracks political violence and Americans’ perceptions of it.

title: Graduates Reset Ambitions in Pursuit of First Jobs
summary: Young people aiming to build careers are entering fields they had not considered to find their footing.

title: Stephen Colbert Gets Ready to Hang It Up
summary: His late-night show ends next month after 11 seasons. He has lots of feelings.

title: Offbeat Obituaries Celebrate Loss With Levity (and Brutal Honesty)
summary: Irreverent tributes filled with unvarnished truths and funny anecdotes, which run counter to more somber traditional obituaries, have gained attention for “how alive they feel,” a researcher said.

title: After an Assassination Attempt, a Grim Political Math Sets In
summary: Assassination attempts against sitting presidents have tended to compound their political problems and isolate them from the public.

title: Former N.B.A. Player Pleads Guilty in Gambling Scheme
summary: Damon Jones is among more than 30 people who were charged last fall in two sweeping indictments that targeted illegal gambling involving current and former N.B.A. players.

title: Texas Lawmakers Question Camp Mystic’s Owners Over Deadly Flood
summary: A state senator told the owners of the camp, where 28 people died, that they would no longer be allowed to operate the camp “if I can have anything to say with that.”

title: Internet Restrictions Spur Russians to Openly Question Putin’s Moves
summary: From beauty influencers to the token political opposition, Russians are openly questioning President Vladimir V. Putin’s moves to hamstring access.
